title: Kata: Rename a Resource Without Destroying It

problem.statement: You renamed the Terraform resource fw_nat_rule.vpn_exempt to fw_nat_rule.ra_vpn_nat_exemption to meet the naming standard. Without extra code, 'terraform plan' now shows 1 to destroy / 1 to add — which would drop every VPN session. Fill in the block that tells Terraform the resource moved, so the plan shows zero changes.
problem.use_case: Terraform tracks resources by address (type + name) in state — a rename is a new address, which plans as destroy+create of real infrastructure. The moved block records the rename in code (reviewable in the PR), unlike 'terraform state mv' which mutates state out-of-band.

hints: Terraform 1.1+ has a top-level block specifically for declaring that a resource's address changed.
The block needs a 'from' (old address) and a 'to' (new address).

solution.explanation: A moved block declares the rename in code so state follows the new address and the plan is a no-op: moved { from = fw_nat_rule.vpn_exempt  to = fw_nat_rule.ra_vpn_nat_exemption }. ('terraform state mv' achieves the same but outside code review.)
